title: ProductionSeeder reports missing required credentials but still exits successfully
reasoning: The seeder prints an error and returns from run(), but does not throw or otherwise mark the Artisan command as failed. Deployment/bootstrap automation can therefore continue after a successful exit code even though the required super-admin account was not created.
reproduction: Run php artisan db:seed --class=ProductionSeeder with app.admin_email or app.admin_password unset, then check the shell exit code. The code path returns normally after printing the error.
recommendation: Make missing required production credentials a hard failure, such as throwing a RuntimeException after printing the message, and update the command test to assert a non-zero failure status.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: ProductionSeederTest checks the output text and user count for missing email/password, but it does not assert that the Artisan command fails with a non-zero status.
suggestedRegressionTest: Update the missing-email and missing-password tests to assert the seeder command fails and no admin user is created.
minimumFixScope: Change the missing-credential branch in ProductionSeeder and tighten the two existing tests.
